title: Solid medium fungal culture extraction
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"raafm","text":"This protocol describes how to obtain a fungal culture dry extract in solid medium for biological testing or LC-MS analysis.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"ukdqx","text":"Use nitrile gloves, safety goggles and lab coat.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"lqlu7","text":"HPLC- grade Methyl Ethyl Ketone (MEK)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"sotla","text":"250mL glass bottle with screw cap","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"kp0es","text":"Scalpel and blades","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ux21p","text":"Funnel","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dxwlx","text":"Decanting funnel","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"uyrxf","text":"Round-bottomed flask","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c1bql","text":"Rotary evaporator","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bpldx","text":"Pastor pipettes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"nu9nb","text":"5mL glass tubes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4tfbu","text":"Evaporator (centrifugal)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"iisoy","text":"-20 °C freezer","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: On the following day, the bottle content is poured into a decanting funnel fitted with a funnel to prevent the transfer of agar pieces. The liquid aqueous phase formed during maceration is discarded and the organic phase, on top, is transferred to a round-bottomed flask. (dMEK=0.8) The organic phase is evaporated to dryness with a rotary evaporator. The extract is then taken up with few mL of MEK and transferred in a weighed 5 mL-glass tube with a Pastor pipette. The tube is evaporated to dryness with a centrifugal evaporator. The extract is weighed and preserved at -20°C.

Fungi cultures are grown in 14.5 cm diameter Petri dishes, which hold 50 mL of culture medium. Using a scalpel, squares measuring approximately 1 cm² are cut out and transferred to a 250 mL glass bottle. 50 mL of MEK are added and the bottle is closed. The bottle is placed on a rotary shaker at room temperature for approximately 20 hours (120 rpm).
creator: Clémence ROHÉE
